records.ety: [OE. pacche; of uncertain origin, perh. for placche; cf. Prov. E. platch patch, LG. plakk, plakke.]

defs: A piece of cloth, or other suitable material, sewed or otherwise fixed upon a garment to repair or strengthen it, esp. upon an old garment to cover a hole.
quotes: Patches set upon a little breach.

defs: A small piece of anything used to repair a breach; as, a patch on a kettle, a roof, etc.
A small piece of black silk stuck on the face, or neck, to hide a defect, or to heighten beauty.
quotes: Your black patches you wear variously.

defs: A piece of greased cloth or leather used as wrapping for a rifle ball, to make it fit the bore.
Fig.: Anything regarded as a patch; a small piece of ground; a tract; a plot; as, scattered patches of trees or growing corn.
quotes: Employed about this patch of ground.

defs: A block on the muzzle of a gun, to do away with the effect of dispart, in sighting.
A paltry fellow; a rogue; a ninny; a fool.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Patched (pcht); p. pr. & vb. n. Patching.]

defs: To mend by sewing on a piece or pieces of cloth, leather, or the like; as, to patch a coat.
To mend with pieces; to repair with pieces festened on; to repair clumsily; as, to patch the roof of a house.
To adorn, as the face, with a patch or patches.
quotes: Ladies who patched both sides of their faces.

defs: To make of pieces or patches; to repair as with patches; to arrange in a hasty or clumsy manner; -- generally with up; as, to patch up a truce.
